text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Maria Elena Reyes
Date of Birth: [on file]
Medical Record Number: [on file]
Ordering Physician: Breast Surgery / Breast Oncology
Pathologist: [Attending Pathologist, on file]
Specimen Received: [date]
Report Date: [date]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: LEFT BREAST MASS, 10 O'CLOCK POSITION - ULTRASOUND-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY (14-gauge, multiple cores)

Specimen B: LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE, LEVEL I - FINE NEEDLE ASPIRATION (FNA), CYTOLOGY

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The patient is a 45-year-old premenopausal woman presenting with a palpable mass in the left upper outer breast, clinically estimated at 3.2 cm. Diagnostic mammogram demonstrated a 3.4 cm irregular spiculated mass in the left upper outer quadrant with architectural distortion (BI-RADS 5). Ultrasound confirmed a 3.2 x 2.8 x 2.5 cm hypoechoic irregular mass at the 10 o'clock position, 4 cm from the nipple. One morphologically abnormal left axillary lymph node with cortical thickening to 8 mm and loss of fatty hilum was identified on axillary ultrasound, suspicious for nodal involvement. Ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy of the left breast mass and fine needle aspiration of the left axillary lymph node performed under ultrasound guidance for pathologic diagnosis. A radiopaque clip was deployed in the breast biopsy site at the time of the procedure.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Received in formalin labeled with patient name, medical record number, and "left breast mass, 10 o'clock." The specimen consists of seven cylindrical tan-white to tan-gray core needle biopsy fragments ranging in length from 0.4 cm to 1.7 cm, with an aggregate length of approximately 1.4 cm. The cores are firm in consistency, with a glistening cut surface and no grossly apparent necrosis. Representative cores are submitted in entirety in cassette A1 for routine hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining and ancillary immunohistochemical studies. A biopsy clip artifact (metallic echogenic artifact) is not grossly identified in the submitted tissue.

Specimen B: Received as two air-dried and two alcohol-fixed smears labeled "left axillary lymph node FNA," along with a cell block preparation. Smears are cellular and bloody. Cell block prepared as cassette B1.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A - Left Breast Core Needle Biopsy:

Sections of the core biopsy material show fragments of fibroadipose and fibrous breast stroma with foci of benign ductal epithelium. The majority of the cores are extensively replaced by a high-grade invasive carcinoma. The neoplastic cells are arranged predominantly in solid nests, sheets, and single infiltrating cords with only rare and abortive tubular structures identified (tubule score 3/3). The neoplastic cells are large and polygonal with moderate to abundant amounts of pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Nuclei are markedly enlarged, vesicular, and pleomorphic with irregular contours, coarsely clumped chromatin, and conspicuous often cherry-red macronucleoli; this corresponds to a high nuclear grade (nuclear score 3/3). Mitotic figures are readily identified and numerous, counting greater than 20 per 10 high-power fields in the most active areas, including atypical mitoses (mitotic score 3/3). The combined Nottingham histologic grade is 9/9 (Grade 3, poorly differentiated). The overall histologic pattern, cytomorphology, and immunophenotype (see below) are diagnostic of invasive carcinoma of no special type (invasive ductal carcinoma, NOS), grade 3.

Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocytes (TILs): A brisk stromal lymphocytic infiltrate is present, predominantly composed of lymphocytes and lesser plasma cells distributed within the tumor stroma between tumor cell nests. TIL density is estimated at approximately 40% (moderate-to-high, per International TIL Working Group criteria). No intraepithelial TILs are noted.

Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS): No ductal carcinoma in situ is identified within the submitted core biopsy tissue.

Lymphovascular Invasion (LVI): Unequivocal lymphovascular invasion is present, identified as cohesive clusters of carcinoma cells within endothelium-lined vascular spaces at the periphery of the invasive tumor, confirmed on immunohistochemical staining with D2-40 (podoplanin) and CD31, which highlights lymphatic and vascular channels containing tumor emboli.

Necrosis: Focal central comedonecrosis-type necrosis is present within some of the larger solid tumor nests.

Background Breast Parenchyma: The non-neoplastic breast parenchyma shows fibrocystic changes with mild columnar cell change. No atypical ductal hyperplasia is present in non-neoplastic areas.

Specimen B - Left Axillary Lymph Node FNA Cytology:

Smears and cell block sections show highly cellular material. Individually dispersed and loosely cohesive clusters of large atypical epithelioid cells are identified with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, irregular nuclear membranes, prominent nucleoli, and brisk mitotic activity. The cytomorphologic features are consistent with metastatic carcinoma, morphologically similar to the left breast primary. Lymphoid background is present but markedly reduced, consistent with replacement of nodal architecture by metastatic carcinoma. No discrete lymphoid follicular fragments are identified.

Conclusion, Specimen B: Positive for malignant cells, consistent with metastatic carcinoma (see note).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Immunohistochemistry (Specimen A - performed on FFPE core biopsy tissue at [Laboratory Name]):

  Estrogen Receptor (ER) [clone SP1, Ventana]:
    Result: NEGATIVE
    Percentage of positive nuclei: 0%
    Intensity: No staining
    Allred Score: 0/8 (proportion score 0 + intensity score 0)
    Interpretation: Negative (ASCO/CAP 2020 guidelines: <1% = negative)

  Progesterone Receptor (PR) [clone 1E2, Ventana]:
    Result: NEGATIVE
    Percentage of positive nuclei: 0%
    Intensity: No staining
    Allred Score: 0/8
    Interpretation: Negative (ASCO/CAP 2020 guidelines: <1% = negative)

  HER2 [clone 4B5, Ventana]:
    Result: 1+ (NEGATIVE)
    Pattern: Faint, incomplete membrane staining in a small proportion of tumor cells
    Interpretation: Negative by IHC (score 1+). ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines: 1+ = negative; no reflex FISH required based on IHC score of 1+. However, HER2 FISH was performed per ordering physician request (see below).

  HER2 Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH) [Vysis PathVysion HER2 DNA Probe Kit]:
    Result: NOT AMPLIFIED
    HER2 signals per cell (mean): 2.4
    CEP17 signals per cell (mean): 2.0
    HER2/CEP17 ratio: 1.2 (normal; ratio <2.0 = not amplified)
    HER2 copy number: 2.4 copies/cell (not increased)
    Interpretation: HER2 negative, non-amplified by FISH (Group 1 criteria; ASCO/CAP 2018)

  Ki-67 Proliferation Index [clone MIB-1, DAKO]:
    Result: 85% of tumor cell nuclei positive (estimated by visual scoring in the most representative area of staining; automated image analysis confirms 83%)
    Interpretation: High proliferative index

  PD-L1 [22C3 antibody, DAKO PharmDx assay, FDA-approved companion diagnostic]:
    Combined Positive Score (CPS): 18
    Scoring: PD-L1 staining observed in tumor cells (partial, weak-to-moderate membranous/cytoplasmic), tumor-associated macrophages, and lymphocytes within the tumor stroma.
    Interpretation: POSITIVE (CPS >/= 10; threshold for pembrolizumab eligibility per KEYNOTE-522 / FDA approval in TNBC)
    Note: This assay was performed and interpreted in a CAP-accredited laboratory using the validated 22C3 pharmDx kit.

  D2-40 (Podoplanin) / CD31 [dual stain, confirmatory for LVI]:
    Result: Highlights lymphovascular spaces containing tumor emboli, confirming lymphovascular invasion as noted in microscopic description.

PEND: Comprehensive genomic profiling (Foundation Medicine FoundationOne CDx) has been ordered on FFPE tissue from this core biopsy. Results will be reported as an addendum to this report when finalized (estimated turnaround time 10-14 business days).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A: LEFT BREAST, 10 O'CLOCK POSITION - ULTRASOUND-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

  - INVASIVE CARCINOMA OF NO SPECIAL TYPE (INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA, NOS)
  - Histologic Grade: GRADE 3 (Nottingham score 9/9)
      Tubule formation: 3/3 (minimal/absent)
      Nuclear pleomorphism: 3/3 (marked)
      Mitotic rate: 3/3 (>20 mitoses per 10 HPF)
  - Estrogen Receptor (ER): NEGATIVE (0%, Allred score 0/8)
  - Progesterone Receptor (PR): NEGATIVE (0%, Allred score 0/8)
  - HER2: NEGATIVE (IHC 1+; FISH HER2/CEP17 ratio 1.2, not amplified)
  - Ki-67: 85% (HIGH)
  - PD-L1 (22C3, CPS): 18 (POSITIVE; CPS >/= 10)
  - Lymphovascular Invasion: PRESENT (confirmed by D2-40/CD31 IHC)
  - Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocytes (TILs): Approximately 40% (brisk)
  - Ductal Carcinoma In Situ: NOT IDENTIFIED in submitted cores
  - Molecular Subtype: TRIPLE-NEGATIVE BREAST CANCER (TNBC)
  - Comprehensive Genomic Profiling: PENDING (addendum to follow)

SPECIMEN B: LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE, LEVEL I - FINE NEEDLE ASPIRATION:

  - POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS, CONSISTENT WITH METASTATIC CARCINOMA
    (Morphologically consistent with metastatic breast carcinoma, corresponding to the left breast primary.)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NOTE / COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This is a high-grade, triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma (ER-/PR-/HER2-) with a markedly elevated Ki-67 proliferative index (85%), confirmed lymphovascular invasion, and a brisk TIL response (approximately 40%). PD-L1 expression by the 22C3 companion diagnostic assay yields a Combined Positive Score of 18, exceeding the established threshold of 10 for checkpoint inhibitor eligibility in early-stage TNBC. The left axillary FNA is positive for metastatic carcinoma, indicating at minimum cN1 disease. Clinical staging in the context of imaging findings is cT2N1M0. Comprehensive genomic profiling results are pending and will be appended as a supplemental addendum to this pathology report.

This report has been reviewed and electronically signed by the attending pathologist of record.

[Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CAP ACCREDITATION: This laboratory is accredited by the College of American Pathologists (CAP). IHC assays were performed in a CLIA-certified laboratory.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
